mnemonics.title: COCOMO Modes
mnemonics.content: OEE - Organic = Easy, Embedded = Extra hard: Organic = Familiar + Small team, Semi-detached = Medium team, Embedded = Hard + Constraints

mnemonics.title: 5 FP Types
mnemonics.content: I O Q E F: Inputs, Outputs, Queries (Inquiries), External files, Internal files - Remember: 'IOQE File types'

mnemonics.title: 15 COCOMO Cost Drivers
mnemonics.content: RELY (Reliability), DATA (Database size), CPLX (Complex), TIME (Exec time constraint), STOR (Main storage), VIRT (Virtual machine), TURN (Computer turnaround), ACAP (Analyst capability), AEXP (Applications experience), PCAP (Programmer capability), VEXP (Virtual machine experience), LEXP (Language experience), MODP (Modern programming), TOOL (Use of software tools), SCED (Required development schedule)

mnemonics.title: COCOMO II Models
mnemonics.content: AEP: Application Composition (early), Early Design (proto), Post-Architecture (detailed) - Remember: 'Always Eat Pizza'

acronyms.usage: Parametric effort estimation model based on program size
Program size measurement in thousands of lines
Unit of effort measurement in software estimation
14 factors used to compute CAF in FP analysis

visualTips.visualization: Draw triangle: KLOC at base, Effort = a × KLOC^b as sides, Time = c × Effort^d as top - bigger base (KLOC) multiplies exponentially going up
Draw bell curve tilted right - effort rises quickly, peaks at 35% of schedule, then declines gradually - shows Putnam model effort distribution over time
Picture 5 boxes: EI=Input forms, EO=Reports, EQ=Search screens, ILF=Master files, EIF=Reference files - each gets complexity weight (Simple/Average/Complex)
Ladder concept: Organic at bottom (easy), Semi-detached middle, Embedded at top (hard) - each level adds complexity

analogies.analogy: Like compound interest - b=1.05 means small projects are economical, b=1.20 means large projects face 'inflation' of effort due to complexity
Like counting rooms in a house: Inputs=doorways, Outputs=windows, Files=rooms - each has size, then adjust for house complexity (GSCs)
Like estimating travel time by remembering similar past trips - adjusts for known differences in traffic, weather, vehicle
Like cooking: first time takes long, second time faster - each repetition reduces time by similar percentage
